[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2022-04-22
[TITLE]: PTI Power Show In Lahore: Glimpses From Imran Khan’s Jalsa [Pictures] - Lens
[METADATA/INTRO]: Ever since the National Assembly’s No-Confidence voting saw Imran Khan being ousted from the PM’s office, the nation has rallied out in support of the skipper, rejecting what they call the ‘imported government’. Millions have come out of their homes,...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Imran Khan’s Lahore Jalsa was quite the power show. According to reports, over 60,000 people were present at the venue. That’s not even counting the people gathered around the screens. This power show in the historical Minar-e-Pakistan grounds comes after a huge turnout in Peshawar and Karachi. Kaptaan further revealed plans to head to Islamabad next. 
 Lahoris embraced the PTI narrative with enthusiasm. Men, women, children, and some even in wheelchairs joined in, carrying PTI and Pakistan flags, banners, and posters. Some were even donning kurtas printed with Imran Khan’s face, their faces painted with stripes in the party’s red and green colors. Here have a look at some of the glimpses from last night’s power show.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: Unknown Date
[TITLE]: In pictures: PTI's power show at Minar-e-Pakistan
[METADATA/INTRO]: Summary With thousands of workers in attendance, the jalsa is being viewed as PTI’s show of strength in Lahore. Photo: OINN (Web Desk) – Gearing up for the general elections, Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf held a massive political gathering at the Minar-e-Pakistan...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Summary With thousands of workers in attendance, the jalsa is being viewed as PTI’s show of strength in Lahore. Photo: OINN 
 (Web Desk) – Gearing up for the general elections, Pakistan Tehreek-e-Insaf held a massive political gathering at the Minar-e-Pakistan in Lahore on Sunday. 
 With thousands of workers in attendance, the jalsa is being viewed as PTI’s show of strength in Lahore – the political hub of the ruling PML-N, right before elections. The top tier leadership of the party was present at the gathering where the party chief Imran Khan delivered a long speech detailing not just his professional, personal and political struggles but also presenting his 11-point agenda.
